checks_reviewed.evidence: Report covers SoT basis, all six SOUL roles, repo-local skills, custom agents, pod-native skill inventory, role-specific mappings, runtime-surface boundaries, durable handoff, and the recommendation for a new post-bootstrap pod-native role workflow skill.

checks_reviewed.command: nl -ba AGENTS.md; nl -ba REPO_OPERATIONS.md; nl -ba PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT.md
checks_reviewed.evidence: Root sources support the report's distinction between pod-native /workspace/skills, repo-local .agents skills, .codex agents, Codex-only repo work in pods, required gates, and local-vs-external proof limits.

All six active SOUL roles were reviewed. The report's role coverage and role-specific skill/agent mappings match the SOUL responsibilities and boundaries, including Mobile Architect's lack of a dedicated repo-local skill.

Current pod-native skill set is setup/readiness oriented and does not include the proposed codex-role-workflow or role-codex-cli-workflow skill.
Durable handoff through GitHub branch/commit/PR and docs/plans/work-units/<work-unit-id>/ artifacts supports the report's pod-isolated handoff analysis.
Only policy-language references to secrets/tokens were found; no apparent secret values were present.
This review target is a read-only analysis report with no source implementation, runtime skill, validator, eval, hook, app, or API changes under review.

No Codex runtime implementation artifact was changed in the reviewed target; this remains required for any later implementation under .agents, .codex, evals, scripts, or mobile-app-dev-team runtime paths.
No mobile UI/runtime behavior was changed or reviewed.

residual_risks: The target report is untracked in git status, so it is not durable branch/PR evidence until intentionally added.
This review did not prove live OpenClaw/OrbStack pod behavior, GitHub branch protection, EAS, Stitch, mobile-mcp device behavior, Jira, Confluence, or other external platform state.
If the recommendation is implemented later, tests/evals/validator assertions must precede or accompany the implementation, and the applicable AGENTS.md gates must pass before PR readiness.
